grant_title: Youth Violence Prevention Impact in Maryland Communities
eligible_applicant_type: For profit corporations other than small businesses
Native American Tribal Organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments)
Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education
Nonprofits that do not have a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education
Public and State controlled institutions of higher education
geographic_restrictions: United States (All), Canada (All), US Territories (All), International (including Israel)
project_area: Youth violence prevention, conflict resolution, mentorship, community building, and law enforcement collaboration
demographic_requirements: None explicitly stated; applicants must demonstrate commitment to preventing youth violence in Maryland communities.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include local schools, community organizations, law enforcement agencies, nonprofits, for-profit corporations, tribal organizations, and small businesses. Collaborative proposals are favored. Applicants must address local youth violence dynamics and propose viable intervention strategies.
